| **" Outside groups have reserved $3 million in television time to begin flooding Florida airwaves in support of U.S. Sen. Marco Rubio"** _\-- Politico, 7/6/16_ Here's the latest, all:
1. After Marco Rubio broke his promise to run for Senate again, we had our strongest month ever for online fundraising due to your grassroots support!

2. But this week, _Cook Political Report_ wrote that the odds here in Florida are now deadlocked at 50-50.

3. And tomorrow the US Chamber is launching a HUGE $1.5 million attack ad against Patrick. (Plus, a GOP Super PAC has another $1.5 million buy set up for next week!)

You know we can't sit back and let them drown us out on the airwaves. **We set up a Rapid Response fund, but we 've only got 24 hours left to get to our $10,000 goal -- and if we don't, our chances to turn Florida blue could be lost.**

[**We urgently need your help. Will you please contribute now?**](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2c37/738133e4/362f9927/7f82bf05/1447347491/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160707?refcode=pme160706_a&recurring=true&amount=25")

I hate to be the bearer of bad news here, but the insane spending behind these attacks is EXACTLY what Republicans hoped for when they convinced Rubio to get back into this race.

You know what else they're hoping for? That you sit this one out, and don't help us fight back against their nasty smear campaign. [**Prove them wrong and chip in to our Rapid Response fund -- we need to raise $4,128 before midnight tomorrow >>**](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2c37/738133e4/362f9927/7f82bf05/1447347491/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160707?refcode=pme160706_a&recurring=true&amount=25")

Thanks,

Josh Wolf
Campaign Manager
Murphy for Florida

name: Ben Carson
subject: If you needed any reminder, this is it:
body:
---
| | [](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/36308792:sD8wUJY0N:m:1:876315805:B7109DCED6E311DB6B49B8A7862CDDD0:r)
---
* * *

Peter,

If you needed any reminder why believers, united by the conviction of our faith, must pledge to vote and participate in our democracy, this is it...

In Iowa, the Fort Des Moines Church of Christ welcomes everyone to attend its services. But according to the Iowa Civil Rights Commission, this is not enough. The Commission has decided to force the church, by law, to use transgender bathrooms.

Beyond that, and what's perhaps even more egregious, is that this action would prohibit pastors from preaching about God's design of male and female, as taught in the book of Genesis.

The church is now fighting back and has filed a lawsuit against the Commission, declaring that their freedom of religion is being violated by an overreaching government.

People of good faith can have disagreements, but where does this stop? The fact is, I have a hard time thinking of many accommodations that seem to satisfy the rigid, anti-religious Left.

The mission of My Faith Votes is to inspire and motivate believers to act on their faith by casting an informed vote based on a biblical worldview. 25 million Christians registered to vote did not do so in 2012, and every day we are living with the consequences.

This story from Iowa is a stark reminder that our Presidential campaign is not the only campaign that matters. We have a lot of work to do everywhere, at every level.

The good news is that we have a bold plan to reach 45 million people over the next 120 days, calling Christians to resist apathy and discouragement, and to change our culture for Christ through their vote.

[**I hope that you will help My Faith Votes in our mission by making a secure, tax-deductible donation. We cannot do our work without your support.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/36308792:sD8wUJY0N:m:1:876315805:B7109DCED6E311DB6B49B8A7862CDDD0:r)

Thank you for your time and generosity.

Sincerely,

Ben Carson

**

subject: Trump released his fundraising numbers. Here are two things to know:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Trump is catching up with us. | Friend --

Yesterday, Donald Trump released his fundraising numbers -- with the help of the RNC, he raised $51 million in June.

Two key things to note here:

1. That’s not chump change. That’s real money -- and it's exactly what Trump needs to keep growing his campaign or to start running attack ads against Hillary.
2. That number also shows the breadth of his support. He had more than 400,000 people give -- and 94 percent of his donors gave $200 or less.

**[That pace puts Trump in the same orbit as this campaign, and we need to separate ourselves again. Will you chip in $1 right now?](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MjU5MjE4MzYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQwOTA5OTUzS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

I can’t tell you how grateful I am for everything that supporters like you did in June to power this campaign -- but even with all of our success, these latest numbers from Trump show that he's catching up with what we're able to do. Now we need to show how we can respond to a challenge like this.

**Will you chip in $1 right now?**
[](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjU5MjE4MzYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQwOTA5OTUzS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Dennis

Dennis Cheng
National Finance Director
Hillary for America

That number also shows the breadth of his support. He had more than 400,000 people give -- and 94 percent of his donors gave $200 or less. I can’t tell you how grateful I am for everything that supporters like you did in June to power this campaign -- but even with all of our success, these latest numbers from Trump show that he's catching up with what we're able to do. Now we need to show how we can respond to a challenge like this. Will you chip in $1 right now? Thank you, Dennis Dennis Cheng National Finance Director Hillary for America | Donald Trump is the presumptive Republican nominee. This is the only team that can stop him -- chip in $1 to help take Trump on: | **** | | Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1.5 million donors committed to electing Hillary Clinton (and keeping Donald Trump out of the White House). name: Russ Feingold
subject: Your page is loading
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
I'm listening to Wisconsinites -- not to Time Warner or AT&T. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf436a/1c469400/6136909/356cb27/750220750/VEsH/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Does this look familiar to you? [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf436a/1c469400/6136909/356cb27/750220750/VEsE/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema") As I've met with people across rural Wisconsin, one thing has become abundantly clear: We need better, faster broadband internet. But my opponent, Senator Johnson, is choosing to listen to the needs of companies like Charter, Time Warner Cable, and AT&T -- not to the needs of Wisconsinites. In fact, Senator Johnson was one of six senators to sign a letter to the FCC urging the commission to slow things down, and define broadband internet at just 4 mbps, down from the new and improved standard of 25 mbps. That reclassification would help Charter and Time Warner make money, but it would hurt students and businesses across Wisconsin. **[Let's treat broadband internet as a utility like we do electricity, and work to provide high internet speeds to all areas of the state. Sign your name here if you agree.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf436a/1c469400/6136909/356cb27/750220750/VEsF/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")** Right now, broadband service is often expensive, slow, and sometimes only offered by one company with no other option. This problem is even worse in rural areas, where broadband is usually more expensive and slower than in urban areas. Yet as I listen to Wisconsinites across the state, it's rural Wisconsinites who are telling me about the fundamental educational and economic necessity of broadband. I've heard from students whose home internet service is so spotty that, late at night, they'll go sit in the parking of the local library to get WiFi signal -- just so they can send in a job application or finish a test. For small business owners, slow broadband can quite literally result in lost revenue, or prevent significant growth. This is a no-brainer for anyone who isn't taking orders from giant telecom corporations: We need to strengthen our rural economies by giving them the tools to succeed and thrive in a 21st century economy. We can start by treating broadband as the essential economic engine that it is. **[Add your name here to support treating broadband as a public utility like electricity.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf436a/1c469400/6136909/356cb27/750220750/VEsC/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")** Sincerely, Russ Feingold | [ADD YOUR NAME »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf436a/1c469400/6136909/356cb27/750220750/VEsD/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&source=ema_2016-07-07_broadband&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")

subject: Join Hillary in Philly as she shatters another glass ceiling
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
18 million cracks later | Friend --

When I think about what it will be like to be there when the Democratic Party nominates a woman for the highest office in the land for the very first time, I think about what it felt like when Hillary conceded the primary, all the way back in 2008.

Honestly? We were pretty distraught. It was a hard-fought race, and we had gotten so close. But one thing Hillary gave us, at least, was a little hope:
> “Although we weren't able to shatter that highest, hardest glass ceiling this time, thanks to you, it's got about 18 million cracks in it, and the light is shining through like never before, filling us all with the hope and the sure knowledge that the path will be a little easier next time.”
Being one of those 18 million cracks made all the difference -- and it meant that the way was a little clearer for us this time.

Not that this path has ever felt easy. This team has struggled and fought, not just for Hillary, but for the rights of women all over this country to break through their own glass ceilings. Seeing her nomination at the Democratic convention this year -- and knowing what a difference this will make for women all over the world -- is an experience you won’t want to miss.

I’m going to be so proud to be there, and the campaign is setting aside funds for a supporter and guest to join us. Will you add your name for a chance to meet Hillary at the convention this month? **Let’s make history together -- it’s going to be unforgettable:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjU5Mjg3MzcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQwOTk3NTc4S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Mini

Mini Timmaraju
Women's Outreach Director
Hillary for America

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: did you see this?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
|
>
>
> Friend, just two weeks since our Democratic sit-in on the House floor Republicans are up to the same old games. [**Please add your name and help us demand Speaker Ryan act NOW to call a vote on commonsense gun legislation.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91d83/70cdc2ba/47fccced/3970db2/2900554054/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/demand-congress-act-on-no-fly-no-buy-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Please+add+your+name+and+help+us+demand+&utm_campaign=em070716&source=em070716")
>
> The House was back in session this week and Republicans continued to block the calls of the American people for an _immediate vote_ on the bipartisan "No Fly No Buy" bill - H.R. 1076 - that would prevent suspected terrorists from purchasing guns.
>
> Instead of calling this vote, Speaker Ryan and his GOP colleagues tried to advance a bill authored by the gun lobby itself! The American people are looking for real solutions -- they don't want a phony NRA-sponsored bill that does nothing to keep our communities safe.
>
> [**Please, add your name right now demanding the GOP schedule a real vote on H.R. 1076, the 'No Fly No Buy' gun safety bill.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91d83/70cdc2ba/47fccced/3970db3/2900554054/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/demand-congress-act-on-no-fly-no-buy-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+FIRSTNAMEPlease+add+your+name+right+now+&utm_campaign=em070716&source=em070716")
>
> Just last night the manager of slain Baltimore rapper Lor Scoota was himself shot down and killed near Druid Hill Park in Northwest Baltimore. These senseless acts of violence have become all too familiar in communities across our country and I am committed to getting action to address this crisis.
>
> But we can't make headway without your help, friend. It's clear Speaker Ryan and the Republicans have sold out to the NRA. They'd rather pander to the gun lobby than prevent suspected terrorists from buying guns.
>
> [**Please add your name here to our petition telling Paul Ryan and House Republicans we want real action NOW against gun violence.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91d83/70cdc2ba/47fccced/3970db0/2900554054/VEsF/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/demand-congress-act-on-no-fly-no-buy-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+Please+add+your+name+here+to+our+petitio&utm_campaign=em070716&source=em070716")
>
> Thank you for joining this effort. It's important.
>
> My colleagues and I will not stop until we get a vote.
>
> Chris

>
>
---

We're getting close, but we're not there yet. We have $1,876 to raise before midnight. **YOUR contribution is needed to put us over the top. **I'm biting my nails -- we cannot afford to miss this deadline.

[**Can you chip in $5 or more NOW to help Patrick defeat Rubio and turn the Senate blue?**](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2c32/738133e4/3620b2ed/7f82b2ec/3427952178/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/pme160707pm?refcode=pme160707pm_b&recurring=true&amount=25")
